MemberSignature.Value: public BinaryData Template { get; }
.property instance class System.BinaryData Template
Public ReadOnly Property Template As BinaryData
member this.Template : BinaryData

Docs.summary:
The template content. Use this element to pass the template syntax directly in the request rather than link to an existing template. It can be a JObject or well-formed JSON string. Use either the templateLink property or the template property, but not both.
To assign an object to this property use .
To assign an already formatted json string to this property use .
Examples:
BinaryData.FromObjectAsJson("foo"). Creates a payload of "foo".
BinaryData.FromString("\"foo\""). Creates a payload of "foo".
BinaryData.FromObjectAsJson(new { key = "value" }). Creates a payload of { "key": "value" }.
BinaryData.FromString("{\"key\": \"value\"}"). Creates a payload of { "key": "value" }.

MemberSignature.Value: public Azure.ResourceManager.Resources.DeploymentStacks.Models.DeploymentStacksTemplateLink TemplateLink { get; }
.property instance class Azure.ResourceManager.Resources.DeploymentStacks.Models.DeploymentStacksTemplateLink TemplateLink
Public ReadOnly Property TemplateLink As DeploymentStacksTemplateLink
member this.TemplateLink : Azure.ResourceManager.Resources.DeploymentStacks.Models.DeploymentStacksTemplateLink

Docs.summary: The URI of the template. Use either the templateLink property or the template property, but not both.
